payload.answer: These two facts conflict: if Sara is your daughter, she would be your direct child; if Sara is your niece, she would be your sibling’s child. They cannot both be true about the same person under standard family relationships. This needs clarification (e.g., whether 'Sara' is misnamed, whether one statement is incorrect, or whether you meant a different relative).
payload.conflict_classification: Relationship-type conflict (same subject, incompatible roles: daughter vs niece).

entity_resolution.reason: Both statements refer to the same name ('Sara') in the same relationship frame ('my ...'), but there is no explicit identifier to confirm they refer to the same individual; however, the name suggests likely identity.

payload.intent: Explain and handle conflict between two relationship facts about the same named person (Sara).

details: Did not overwrite the existing durable fact; recorded the new relationship statement as a conflicting candidate requiring clarification.

repair_options: Confirm whether 'Sara' refers to the same person as in the existing fact.
If one is incorrect, correct or remove the wrong relationship.
If two different people are named 'Sara', add distinguishing details (e.g., middle initial, nickname, age, or which side of the family).
Clarify the intended relationship (daughter vs niece) and any context (step-/adopted relationships, half-sibling cases, or cultural/colloquial usage).
